typecheck.notes: 119 errors — all pre-existing (documented in AGENTS.md as known technical debt, ~119 errors mostly in browser.py). No new errors introduced by live-stability features.

lint.notes: 34 errors — all pre-existing (documented in AGENTS.md as ~35 known errors, mostly I001 import sorting, F401 unused imports, style issues). No new errors introduced by live-stability features.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Unused 'timezone' import in audit_orchestrator.py (line 4). Changed to use UTC directly but didn't remove the old import.
Inner 'import logging' in _handle_audit_failure's except block. Works as last-resort fallback but inconsistent with module-level imports pattern.
f-string used in logger.critical() call. Per ruff G004 and Python best practices, should use lazy % formatting.
Orchestrator doesn't check provider's summary['status'] for logical all-scenario failures (only catches exceptions). When all scenarios fail without raising, audit may still be marked 'completed'.
rule_engine.py uses hardcoded scenario name strings instead of importing from taxonomy.py, inconsistent with AGENTS.md convention.
Redundant sys.path.insert and os.environ.setdefault in test file (already handled by conftest.py).
Two nearly identical tests (test_create_audit_with_default_scenarios and test_create_audit_with_default_personas) that test the same assertion.
test_full_audit_flow_create_and_get is a weak integration test — POST and GET test different audit objects.
api_test_client fixture duplicates conftest.py's test_client fixture instead of reusing/extending it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add testing convention: 'Test files should NOT duplicate sys.path.insert or os.environ.setdefault from conftest.py. Rely on conftest.py for test environment setup. New test files needing custom fixtures should extend the shared test_client fixture from conftest.py rather than creating parallel fixtures.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: backend/tests/test_audits_api.py duplicates setup from conftest.py (lines 17-18 and fixture lines 37-73). Same pattern seen in test_audits_api.py's api_test_client vs conftest.py's test_client.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen enforcement of scenario name imports: 'In rule_engine.py and audit_orchestrator.py, scenario names used in conditionals/switch statements MUST be imported as constants from taxonomy.py, not used as string literals. Pattern: `from app.core.taxonomy import AUDIT_SCENARIOS` then reference `AUDIT_SCENARIOS["cookie_consent"]["id"]` or define named constants.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: rule_engine.py lines 179, 216, 334 use hardcoded strings 'subscription_cancellation', 'checkout_flow'. audit_orchestrator.py line 341-346 uses string literals. AGENTS.md already says 'NEVER hardcode' but workers still do it.

rejectedObservations.observation: MockBrowserAuditProvider in browser.py still uses legacy 'cancellation_flow' scenario name (6 occurrences).
rejectedObservations.reason: AGENTS.md mission boundary: 'Do NOT modify mock or hybrid provider BEHAVIOR'. These are pre-existing and off-limits. taxonomy.py LEGACY_SCENARIO_MAP provides backward compatibility.
openapi.json appears to be auto-generated. It will be updated when the API schema is regenerated. Not a manual fix target.
